title: Today's Tech Brief: Google's AI Push and a Critical Gogs Bug
summary: Google's AI enshittification and a critical bug in Gogs make headlines
imageAlt: Image from source article for Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately
imageCaption: Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately
imageSourceTitle: Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's tech brief. I've got a few headlines that caught my eye recently. Google's been making some big bets on AI, and not everyone's convinced it's the right move. Meanwhile, a critical bug in a popular open-source Git service has left folks scrambling for a fix. And, as I was digging into these stories, I stumbled upon a few more interesting tidbits that I just had to share.

First up, Google's AI enshittification. According to a report on The Register, Google's been leaning hard into AI, and some are wondering if it's a recipe for disaster. The Chocolate Factory's mission to reshape the web could backfire if users aren't on board. I'm not sure about you, but I get a little nervous when I think about how much AI is influencing my search results. It's like, I want to find what I'm looking for, but I also want to feel like I'm getting a genuine answer, not just some AI-generated fluff.

Next, a critical bug in Gogs, an open-source Git service. Researchers found a remote code execution vulnerability, and an exploit module is already out. The maintainers haven't responded to messages since March, which is just great. If you're using Gogs, you might want to keep an eye on this one. It's a good reminder to stay on top of updates and security patches, even if it feels like a chore.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Google has seriously leaned into AI enshittification lately
No fix yet for critical RCE bug in open-source Git service Gogs - exploit module is out
